# Core Pattern Notes

This reference distills practical points from the supplied sources into a compact checklist.

## Sources
- https://www.ig.com/en/trading-strategies/16-candlestick-patterns-every-trader-should-know-180615
- https://www.strike.money/technical-analysis/types-of-candlesticks-patterns
- https://tradeciety.com/how-to-trade-candlestick-patterns-like-a-pro

## Practical Principles
- Context over pattern name: location and trend matter more than shape alone.
- Confirmation over prediction: many patterns require follow-through candle or breakout.
- Fewer patterns, better execution: focus on a shortlist you can review repeatedly.
- Risk first: set stop and size before entry.

## High-Use Reversal Patterns
- Bullish: hammer, bullish engulfing, morning star, dragonfly doji (with confirmation).
- Bearish: shooting star, bearish engulfing, evening star, gravestone doji (with confirmation).

## High-Use Continuation Patterns
- Marubozu in strong trend.
- Rising Three (bullish), Falling Three (bearish).
- Inside bar continuation in trend direction.

## Indecision Patterns (Need Confirmation)
- Doji
- Spinning top
- Long-legged doji

## Fast Validation Checklist
- Is pattern at meaningful location?
- Is broader trend clear?
- Is there confirmation candle or valid breakout?
- Is stop at invalidation point?
- Is expected reward at least 1.5R?
- Is risk size within plan?
